hypothesis:
## Novel Hypothesis

Incorporating trace amounts of electrochemically inactive framework-forming elements (e.g., B, P, or light transition metals) into Si-based alloy anodes will create rigid polyhedral scaffolds that decouple Li+ migration channel geometry from lithiation state, thereby enabling >2000 mAh/g capacity with <5% channel radius variation across full charge/discharge cycles—a performance unattainable by binary Si-X systems identified in this study.
